findings.title: Sequential cleanup in test helper leaks server on client.close() failure
findings.description: The createConnectedPair helper's cleanup function calls client.close() then server.close() sequentially without try/finally. If client.close() throws, server.close() is never called, leaving the MCP server transport open. Every test in this file uses this helper via try/finally, so a single flaky close would leak the server and potentially hang the test suite. This is the same sequential-cleanup-in-single-block pattern previously found in McpHub.shutdown() (fixed in iter2) and Email.disconnect().

findings.title: JSON.parse on MCP response content without descriptive assertion wrapper
findings.description: Multiple JSON.parse calls (lines 94, 116, 203, 231) parse MCP server response text without try/catch or a descriptive wrapper. If the server returns malformed JSON (e.g., during development when the handler is changed), the test fails with a generic SyntaxError('Unexpected token...') rather than a clear message like 'search_memories returned non-JSON: <actual text>'. This is a test-quality concern rather than a production error handling issue, but it hampers debuggability when tests break.

positive_observations: All 5 test files include clear Test Double rationale comments explaining why real dependencies are stubbed -- consistent with CLAUDE.md testing rules.
mcp-hub tests use try/finally for cleanup in every test case -- disciplined resource lifecycle pattern despite the sequential close issue.
channels-extra tests verify fault isolation (one failing channel does not block others) -- directly tests the allSettled pattern in production code.
persona-engine tests are pure function tests with zero I/O -- clean Functional Core testing, no error handling needed by design.
stage-ui store tests are synchronous-only state management -- error-free-by-design UI layer pattern continues from prior mcp.ts review.

mcp-hub tests include explicit error path testing (line 124-146: 'handles search errors gracefully') -- verifying that production code returns isError:true with descriptive message on dependency failure.
context-engine tests verify multi-source merging orchestration without hitting real LLM -- correct boundary testing approach.
